private.name: ClearSpace
private.rationale: Same sector (Space & Aerospace), same stage (Series A), post-money $400M

data.narrative:
KP Labs, a Series A-stage Space & Aerospace company founded by Grzegorz Kasprowicz, was valued against a peer universe of four same-sector, same/adjacent-stage private companies: Aalyria ($1.3B Series B), ClearSpace ($400M Series A), Hubble Network ($400M Series B), and Sift ($274M Series B).

The peer-median method provided the primary valuation anchor. The median valuation across these four comparables stands at $400M, with a range spanning $274M to $1.3B. This method is applicable given the direct sector and stage alignment of the peer set. Public-comp anchoring using the Space & Aerospace sector's 59.8x median P/S multiple across six public tickers (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) could not be applied, as KP Labs' revenue is not on file—a typical constraint for private company analysis.

The triangulation supports a valuation range of $340M to $460M, centered on the peer median of $400M. The relatively tight range reflects reliance on a single applicable method and modest dispersion within the peer set. Key uncertainties include KP Labs' specific revenue trajectory, customer concentration, and technical differentiation relative to ClearSpace and Hubble Network, both valued at or near the median. Without disclosed financials, precision beyond the $340M–$460M band is not supported by the data.
